headline: Cisco Q3 FY26 blowout: rev $15.8B, EPS $1.06; lifts FY26 AI infra orders target to $9B from $5B; cutting ~4,000 jobs; stock +15% pre-market
summary: Cisco delivered a record Q3: revenue $15.84B (+12% YoY) vs $15.56B consensus; non-GAAP EPS $1.06 vs $1.04. AI infrastructure orders from hyperscalers hit $2.1B in Q3 (vs $0.6B prior-year), bringing YTD AI orders to $5.3B. Management nearly doubled FY26 AI-order target to $9B (from $5B prior). Networking orders +50% YoY, datacenter switching +40%, campus +25%. Raised FY26 revenue guide to $62.8-63.0B (vs $61.6B consensus) and EPS to $4.27-4.29. Announced restructuring of fewer than 4,000 roles (~5% of HC) to fund AI investments. Q4 guide: rev $16.7-16.9B. Silicon One G300 and Splunk ramp both cited as drivers.

headline: US clears Nvidia H200 sales to ~10 Chinese tech giants as Trump-Xi summit opens in Beijing; NVDA +3% overnight
summary: Reuters exclusive: Washington has authorized Nvidia H200 exports to ~10 Chinese buyers including Alibaba, Tencent, ByteDance and JD.com, with distributors Lenovo and Foxconn also cleared. Each approved customer can purchase up to 75,000 H200 chips. Disclosure landed as Trump and Xi opened the Beijing summit (May 14-15) — Jensen Huang joined the delegation last-minute at Trump's invitation, calling it 'one of the most important summits in human history'. Caveat: China is reportedly pressuring domestic firms to defer or block orders pending Beijing review, mirroring the H20 dynamic. Tandem to US-China tariff truce announced May 12 (US duties 145%→30%, China 125%→10%).

headline: Cerebras (CBRS) opens trading today on Nasdaq at $185 IPO — $5.55B raised, 20x oversubscribed; indicated to open ~90% above issue
summary: Cerebras priced its IPO Wednesday night at $185, above the upsized $150-160 range; 30M Class A shares raised $5.55B — the largest Nasdaq debut of 2026. Implied valuation in the high-$60Bs fully diluted. Book reportedly 20x oversubscribed; Morgan Stanley, Citi, Barclays, UBS led. Indicated to open near $350 in early indications. WSE-3 wafer-scale architecture and OpenAI/Amazon customer base position it as the most credible public-market competitor to Nvidia's clustered-GPU stack since Groq. Watch the open for read-through to NVDA (sentiment, public-comp pricing) and to AMD/INTC (alt-AI architecture validation).

headline: Marvell rallies +5% pre-market as Cisco AI read-across hits; BofA lifts PT to $200 (from $125), Goldman to $125 (from $100)
summary: MRVL gapped up ~5% in pre-market on the Cisco AI-orders beat as the networking/optics read-across hit custom ASIC and DSP names. BofA's Vivek Arya raised MRVL PT to $200 (from $125), keeping Buy, citing expanding AI datacenter TAM and Marvell's role in AI networking hardware including supply into Nvidia systems; sets up ahead of MRVL's Q1 FY27 print May 27. Goldman bumped its 12-month target to $125 (from $100) and raised FY27/FY28 EPS estimates ~5%, but kept Neutral. Broader networking/optics tape positive: ANET, AVGO, COHR, LITE all bid.

headline: SK Hynix breaches KRW 2M intraday for first time — closes in on $1T market cap; Samsung +4.5% on AI-memory uplift; KOSPI200 concentration tops 50%
summary: SK Hynix crossed KRW 2,000,000 in pre-market and intraday Thursday for the first time ever as the HBM4 cycle and Nvidia Rubin pull-through extend the AI-memory rally. Samsung Electronics jumped 4.54% to an all-time high KRW 299,500. Bloomberg flagged SK Hynix as on the cusp of $1T market cap. Combined weight of Samsung + SK Hynix in KOSPI200 surpassed 50% for the first time, raising index-concentration risk. KOSPI closed +1.75% at 7,981. SK Hynix briefly overtook Samsung in market cap and forward P/E earlier this week. Reinforces the Tower Semi (May 13) silicon-photonics signal and the broader memory supercycle thesis.

headline: Applied Materials reports Q2 FY26 after the bell — consensus EPS $2.68 on rev $7.69B; HBM and advanced packaging in focus
summary: AMAT prints Q2 FY26 today (4:30 PM ET call). Company guided rev $7.65B +/- $500M; Street at $7.69B implies +8.4% YoY. Consensus EPS $2.68 (+12% YoY). Setup: AI-driven leading-edge logic, HBM DRAM equipment, and advanced packaging are the swing factors after Q1 beat (rev $7.01B, EPS $2.38 vs $2.21 est, +8% one-day reaction). New TSMC AI partnership and $5B EPIC R&D bet are layered narratives. Read-throughs: LRCX, KLAC, ASML, TSM, and HBM names (000660.KS, 005930.KS, MU).

headline: Siemens Energy raises FY26 outlook after record Q2: orders €17.7B, backlog €154B; FY revenue growth lifted to 14-16%, FCF to ~€8B
summary: Siemens Energy (ENR.DE) Q2 FY26 (reported May 12, available in feeds today): revenue €10.3B (+9% comparable), Q2 net income €766M vs €433M prior, EPS €0.88. Record €17.7B orders and €154B backlog. FY26 guide raised across the board: comparable revenue +14-16% (prior +11-13%), margin 10-12% (prior 9-11%), net income ~€4B (prior €3-4B), FCF before tax ~€8B (prior €4-5B). Confirms datacenter-power equipment supercycle read-through to GEV, ETN, ABB and adjacent gear suppliers; aligns with Siemens AG's May 13 Smart Infrastructure 'triple-digit' datacenter order growth call.

headline: Initial jobless claims jump to 211k (vs 199k prior) ahead of April retail sales; tape softens just as Fed digests this week's hot CPI/PPI
summary: Labor Department: initial claims +12k to 211k for the week ending May 9 (vs 205k forecast). Continuing claims +24k to 1,782k. Print follows the +1.4% MoM PPI shock May 13 and +0.6% CPI May 12 — slightly softer labor signal partially offsets the stagflation tone for AI growth multiples. April retail sales due 8:30 AM ET today (forecast +0.5% vs prior +1.7%). Powell's chairmanship ends May 15 (he remains a Fed governor); no scheduled Powell speech today. Treasury yields easing pre-bell; supportive for long-duration tech.

headline: CoreWeave secures ~$6B Jane Street AI-compute commitment plus $1B equity at $109/share; CRWV +3.8% on May 13
summary: CoreWeave (CRWV) disclosed Jane Street is taking ~$6B of AI-cloud capacity plus a $1B common-equity ticket priced at $109/share — a marquee validation of CRWV's neocloud pricing power from a sophisticated quant. Lifts the stock 3.8% on May 13 and partially heals the post-Q1 -13% drawdown (EPS missed though revenue ripped). Counter-narrative: banks are quietly offloading parts of CRWV's AI-datacenter debt exposure, highlighting leverage risk in the neocloud build. Adjacent read-through positive for VRT (cooling), DLR/EQIX (capacity-adjacent), and NVDA (Blackwell pull-through).
